title: TET2 mutation: CHIP versus AML-defining disease
learning_objectives: Distinguish diagnostic, prognostic and clonal markers
clinical_vignette: A 76-year-old man has AML with 28% blasts. NGS shows TET2 mutation VAF 44% and IDH2 mutation VAF 18%. NPM1, FLT3 and TP53 are negative. Karyotype is normal.
question: Which interpretation is most appropriate?
options: A. TET2 alone defines AML with myelodysplasia-related gene mutation.
B. TET2 may represent clonal haematopoiesis or an early cooperating lesion and must be interpreted in context.
C. TET2 mutation proves acute promyelocytic leukaemia.
E. TET2 mutation defines core-binding factor AML.
correct_answer: B. TET2 may represent clonal haematopoiesis or an early cooperating lesion and must be interpreted in context.
explanation: TET2 is a common age-related clonal haematopoiesis gene and may participate in AML pathogenesis, but it is not by itself an AML-defining abnormality. Interpretation requires blast count, morphology, cytogenetics and co-mutations.
frcpath_pearl: Not every mutation in an AML NGS report defines the AML entity.
take_home_message: Molecular findings in AML must be interpreted in diagnostic hierarchy and clinical context.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th Edition
